description:
`worker.log` is still too silent for real operator use: after switching workers to non-interactive `pi --print`, long delegated runs can show almost no activity until final output. That leaves the operator unable to tell whether the worker is progressing, stuck, or waiting.

Scope:
- change delegated worker execution/logging so meaningful activity is streamed during the run instead of only at the end
- prefer a machine-readable mode such as `pi --mode json` when possible so logs can include structured events without overwhelming noise
- keep the log useful for humans by filtering or summarizing event types when appropriate, rather than dumping raw unreadable noise
- add regression coverage for streamed progress appearing during worker execution
title: Stream delegated worker activity so long-running work is observable
